name: Ralph Sutton
abstract: Ralph Earl Sutton (November 4, 1922 – December 30, 2001) was an American jazz pianist born in Hamburg, Missouri. He was a stride pianist in the tradition of James P.

interviews.title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ernest 'Punch' Miller
about_quotes.evidence: I played with him, tell the truth, the whole time I was in New York, because everything I got he called me up and I'd go on with him

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Grosz
about_quotes.evidence: I was just over there and played some with Ralph Sutton, he’s a fine player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Grosz interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ken Peplowski
about_quotes.evidence: Ralph Sutton has been a friend of mine and we’ve played together a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Ken Peplowski interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 10, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: the intermission piano player at Condon's for several years was Ralph Sutton... he hired Ralph to be our piano player at that first party... Ralph Sutton, Cutty Cutshall, Wild Bill Davis son, Edmund Hall, Jack Lesberg on bass... we Ralph Sutton was on piano, and he took Walter Page up, and we made o

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: one of the selections I did was 'You're Driving Me Crazy,' with Bob Wilber on soprano sax; Ralph Sutton on piano... the first group that I sang with at the Village Vanguard was a group made up of, it had Ralph Sutton on piano

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Haggart
about_quotes.evidence: And I’ve been over since then too with a lot of the same guys, Kenny Davern and Yank Lawson and George Masso, and Ralph Sutton, and Jake Hanna.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Haggart interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995
